criteria.label: State the canonical ML definition: systems learn patterns from data to improve task performance.
criteria.example: In one sentence: "We train on labeled emails so the model learns spam cues from data instead of maintaining a manual keyword list."

criteria.label: Name the four staples of a supervised ML problem: features, labels, model, and learning algorithm.
criteria.example: House prices: features = [sqft, bedrooms], label = sold_price, model = linear regression, algorithm = minimize MSE on train rows.

criteria.label: Explain training vs inference and why they are separated in production.
criteria.example: Train nightly on warehouse logs to produce model_v3.pkl; daytime API loads v3 and scores requests in <50ms without running gradient updates.

criteria.label: Describe why held-out evaluation is required and what overfitting means.
criteria.example: Train accuracy 99% and test accuracy 54% on churn → model memorized train ids; fix with simpler model or more data and report test F1 only once.

criteria.label: Place ML within AI and connect training loss to maximum likelihood when appropriate.
criteria.example: ML is a data-driven branch of AI; binary cross-entropy on labels is Bernoulli negative log-likelihood—training performs MLE of spam probability parameters.
